scan-and-fix.prompt: IMPORTANT: Before generating any report, read and follow ALL rules in Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/config/report-rules.md. Scan repos for open issues (bugs, feature requests, enhancements -- all types, not just bugs). First, discover new repos by running `find /q/src -maxdepth 4 -name ".git" -type d` and comparing against the known target repos table. For any new repo found: check if it has uncommitted changes on non-default branches (skip those); if clean, run `git pull` to sync. Then for each known target repo: (1) List all open issues. (2) For each open issue, check if a PR already exists: run `GH_TOKEN=<PAT> gh pr list --repo <owner>/<repo> --search "#<issue_number>" --state all --json number,state,mergedAt,url,title`. If a PR is found and merged, close the issue with reason 'completed' and comment 'Closed -- fixed by PR #N (merged).'. If a PR is found and closed without merge, close the issue with reason 'not planned' and comment 'Closing -- PR #N was closed. See PR for details.'. If a PR is still open, skip the issue. Only proceed to fix if no PR exists. (3) Skip issues already in the attempts log. (4) Pick the highest-priority remaining issue (bugs first, then enhancements, then unlabeled; oldest first within same type), analyze the root cause, implement a fix, and submit a PR. In the scan report, add a 'New Repos Detected' section listing any new repos with their path and a copyable CLI suggestion to add them. If NO new fixes were implemented (no PRs submitted, no code changes pushed -- only stale issue closures or deferrals to open-pr-maintenance), do NOT generate an HTML report. Just output a one-line summary like 'No new bugs to fix. Closed N stale issues, deferred N to open-pr-maintenance.' and exit. Only generate a full report when actual code work was done (PR submitted, fix implemented). Follow the instructions in your agent file. CRITICAL: Do NOT post anything to Teams. Do NOT send Teams messages. Do NOT use Graph API to post to channels. Only generate the HTML report file.
scan-and-fix.description: Scans repos for all open issues (bugs, enhancements, feature requests), discovers new repos, analyzes root cause, creates fix PRs

open-pr-maintenance.prompt: IMPORTANT: Before generating any report, read and follow ALL rules in Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/config/report-rules.md. Maintain all open PRs I authored across both GitHub repos AND Azure DevOps repos. Follow ALL steps in the open-pr-maintenance section of your agent file. IMPORTANT: For jqxcode/* repos, extract the PAT from git remote URL and use GH_TOKEN=$(git remote get-url origin | grep -oP 'ghp_[^@]+') before all gh commands. This includes: (1) List open PRs from GitHub repos (gh pr list) AND ADO repos (teams-modular-packages via ADO MCP). (2) Resolve merge conflicts on any conflicted PRs. (3) Address unresolved review comments. (3b) For ADO PRs in teams-modular-packages: check ALL PR-triggered builds via the PR statuses API, fix prettier formatting errors, create work items for Themis gate failures, retry failed builds via the retry API. (3c) Nudge pending reviewers via the GitOps +reviewready thread. (3d) Detect orphaned PRs where the linked bug is Closed and auto-abandon them. Follow safety rules in your agent file. If NO PRs needed any maintenance, do NOT generate an HTML report -- just output 'No open PRs to maintain.' and exit. Only generate a report when actual work was done. When generating a report, you MUST use the fixed template at Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/templates/bug-killer-pr-maintenance.html. Read the template file, then replace all {{PLACEHOLDER}} values with actual data. Table rows must be sorted by repo then PR number. Result column must show specific state (Action Taken, Waiting for Review, Waiting for Merge, Needs Publishing, Abandoned) not generic No Action. Notes section is removed; use per-repo summaries inside the Summary card instead. Replace {{VO_SUBTITLE}} with: Agent: Bug Killer | Job: open-pr-maintenance | Start: <start time> | Complete: <complete time>. CRITICAL: Do NOT post anything to Teams. Do NOT send Teams messages. Do NOT use Graph API to post to channels. Only generate the HTML report file.
open-pr-maintenance.description: Maintain open PRs: resolve merge conflicts and address review comments

daily-summary.prompt: IMPORTANT: Before generating any report, read and follow ALL rules in Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/config/report-rules.md. Generate a daily summary report for all bug-killer activity from the past 24 hours. Steps: (1) List all HTML files in Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/output/bug-killer/ modified in the last 24 hours (exclude *-latest.html symlinks and the daily-summary files themselves). (2) For each file, extract from the HTML: the issue/PR number, repo name, title, outcome (fixed/skipped/failed), and the PR URL if one was created. (3) Also collect and link reports from ALL other bug-killer jobs (scan-and-fix, open-pr-maintenance) that ran in the past 24h -- the daily summary should be the single report to keep open, linking to all individual reports. Do NOT open individual reports in Edge. (4) You MUST use the fixed template at Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/templates/bug-killer-daily-summary.html. Read the template file, then replace all {{PLACEHOLDER}} values with actual data. Replace {{VO_SUBTITLE}} with: Agent: Bug Killer | Job: daily-summary | Start: <PST timestamp when job started> | Complete: <PST timestamp when report is generated>. Replace {{ACTIVITY_TABLE_ROWS}} with one row per run report, where each row links to the individual run report as a file:/// URL. Columns: Time, Job Type (scan-and-fix / open-pr-maintenance), Repo, Issue/PR, Title, Link to Report. If any runs were skipped or produced no output, replace {{SKIPPED_SUMMARY}} with a brief one-line explanation per skipped run (e.g. 'scan-and-fix: No unattempted issues remaining across all repos'). If nothing was skipped, replace {{SKIPPED_SUMMARY}} with an empty string. If no activity in the past 24h, replace the table with the no-activity message. Replace {{JOB_DURATIONS_CONTENT}} by running: python Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/runner/Get-JobDurations.py --agent bug-killer --days 30 and inserting its output. The output should be formatted as timeline chart data (JSON array of {job, start, end, duration} objects) that the template will render as a visual timeline. Save the report to Q:/src/personal_projects/virtual-office/output/bug-killer/daily-summary-YYYYMMDD.html and also copy as daily-summary-latest.html. (5) The daily-summary report is the single report to open (individual reports should NOT be opened separately). CRITICAL: Do NOT post anything to Teams. Do NOT send Teams messages. Do NOT use Graph API to post to channels. Only generate the HTML report file.
daily-summary.description: Daily summary linking all bug-killer activity reports from the past 24h, opened in Edge Daily tab group
